content.text: Giờ check xem event flow từ agent → adapter thế nào:

Từ screenshot mình thấy 2 vấn đề:

**1. Tool calls "Read File" và "Edit" bị stuck ở trạng thái pending (⏳), không update thành ✅**

Nguyên nhân nằm ở `tool-call-tracker.ts:94`:

```typescript
const isTerminal = meta.status === "completed" || meta.status === "failed";
if (!isTerminal) return;
```

Chỉ check 2 status `completed` và `failed`, nhưng ACP có thể gửi các status khác như `"done"`, `"error"`, `"cancelled"` — những cái này bị skip, message không bao giờ được edit thành ✅.

**2. Tool hiện "Read File" / "Edit" không có file path**

`formatToolSummary` check `lowerName === "read"` nhưng agent gửi tên tool là `"Read File"` (lowercase = `"read file"` ≠ `"read"`), nên rơi vào fallback `🔧 ${name}` không có path.

message.content: vấn để đang làm kiểm tra PR 51 và các tinh nang xem co bị override không doc spec để hiểu /Users/kienduong/Downloads/2026-03-24-message-formatting-v2-design.md

content.content:
1→# Message Formatting v2 Design Spec 2→ 3→**Date:** 2026-03-24 4→**Status:** Approved 5→ 6→## Overview 7→ 8→Three enhancements to the message formatting system: 9→ 10→1. **Agent-side display summaries** — agents provide their own tool call summaries via `_meta.displaySummary`, eliminating the need to maintain tool name mappings for 30+ agents 11→2. **Per-adapter verbosity levels** — `low`, `medium`, `high` control how much detail each adapter shows 12→3. **Noise filtering** — auto-hide or collapse low-value tool calls (directory reads, glob searches) that are internal agent mechanics 13→ 14→## Decisions 15→ 16→| Aspect | Decision | 17→|---|---| 18→| Agent summary source | `_meta.displaySummary` (optional, fallback to current `formatToolSummary`) | 19→| Verbosity scope | Per-adapter (config per channel) | 20→| Default verbosity | `medium` | 21→| Inline content on `medium` | **Removed** — `medium` now shows summary line + viewer links, no inline content. Previously `medium` included truncated content. This is a minor visual change: viewer links replace inline previews, giving a cleaner default. | 22→| Noise filtering | First-match-wins evaluation, `"hide" \| "collapse" \| null` return type | 23→| Breaking changes | `medium` visual output changes (inline content removed → viewer links instead). Config defaults to `"medium"` so no config migration needed. | 24→ 25→## 1. Agent-side Display Summaries 26→ 27→### Problem 28→ 29→`formatToolSummary()` hardcodes 9 Claude-specific tool names (`Read`, `Edit`, `Bash`, etc.). OpenACP supports 30+ agents with different naming: 30→ 31→| Agent | Tool name | Current match? | 32→|---|---|---| 33→| Claude | `Read` | ✅ | 34→| Codex | `read_file` | ❌ → `🔧 read_file` | 35→| Cline | `read_file` | ❌ → `🔧 read_file` | 36→| Cursor | `file_read` | ❌ → `🔧 file_read` | 37→ 38→### Solution 39→ 40→Agents provide summaries via the existing `_meta` freeform field in ACP protocol. No protocol spec change needed. 41→ 42→```typescript 43→// Agent emits tool_call with _meta: 44→{ 45→ sessionUpdate: "tool_call", 46→ toolCallId: "tc-123", 47→ title: "read_file", 48→ kind: "read", 49→ status: "pending", 50→ rawInput: { path: "src/main.ts" }, 51→ _meta: { 52→ displaySummary: "📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)", 53→ displayTitle: "src/main.ts", 54→ displayKind: "read" 55→ } 56→} 57→``` 58→ 59→### Resolution order 60→ 61→**Summary** (used on `medium` and `high`): 62→``` 63→1. meta.displaySummary → use directly (agent-provided) 64→2. formatToolSummary(name, rawInput) → pattern match (current logic) 65→3. Fallback → "🔧 {name}" 66→``` 67→ 68→**Title** (used on `low`): 69→``` 70→1. meta.displayTitle → use directly (agent-provided) 71→2. formatToolTitle(name, rawInput) → extract target (file path, command, query) 72→3. Fallback → name 73→``` 74→ 75→**Kind**: 76→``` 77→1. meta.displayKind → use directly 78→2. event.kind → from ACP event 79→3. Fallback → "other" 80→``` 81→ 82→### MessageTransformer change 83→ 84→Forward `rawInput`, `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, and `displayKind` into `OutgoingMessage.metadata`. 85→ 86→> **Note:** The current `MessageTransformer.transform()` does NOT forward `rawInput` to metadata. 87→> This is a pre-existing bug — `formatToolSummary()` needs `rawInput` to generate smart summaries 88→> (e.g., extracting `file_path` from Read, `command` from Bash). Without this fix, agents that 89→> don't send `_meta.displaySummary` still get generic `🔧 toolName` fallback. 90→> This change fixes the bug AND adds the new `_meta` fields in one pass. 91→ 92→```typescript 93→// In message-transformer.ts, tool_call case: 94→const meta = event.meta as Record | undefined; 95→const metadata: Record = { 96→ id: event.id, 97→ name: event.name, 98→ kind: event.kind, 99→ status: event.status, 100→ content: event.content, 101→ rawInput: event.rawInput, // FIX: was missing — required for formatToolSummary fallback 102→ displaySummary: meta?.displaySummary, 103→ displayTitle: meta?.displayTitle, 104→ displayKind: meta?.displayKind, 105→}; 106→``` 107→ 108→Apply the same `rawInput` fix to the `tool_update` case (same pattern). 109→ 110→### formatToolSummary change 111→ 112→Add `displaySummary` parameter (agent-provided override) and extract a new `formatToolTitle()` for `low` verbosity: 113→ 114→```typescript 115→// Full summary for medium/high: "📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)" 116→export function formatToolSummary( 117→ name: string, 118→ rawInput: unknown, 119→ displaySummary?: string, 120→): string { 121→ if (displaySummary && typeof displaySummary === "string") { 122→ return displaySummary; 123→ } 124→ // ... existing pattern matching logic unchanged 125→} 126→ 127→// Title only for low: "src/main.ts", "pnpm test", "\"TODO\" in src/" 128→export function formatToolTitle( 129→ name: string, 130→ rawInput: unknown, 131→ displayTitle?: string, 132→): string { 133→ if (displayTitle && typeof displayTitle === "string") { 134→ return displayTitle; 135→ } 136→ let args: Record = {}; 137→ try { 138→ if (typeof rawInput === "string") args = JSON.parse(rawInput) as Record; 139→ else if (typeof rawInput === "object" && rawInput !== null) args = rawInput as Record; 140→ } catch { return name; } 141→ 142→ const lowerName = name.toLowerCase(); 143→ 144→ // File operations → file path 145→ if (["read", "edit", "write"].includes(lowerName)) { 146→ return String(args.file_path ?? args.filePath ?? name); 147→ } 148→ // Bash → command (truncated) 149→ if (lowerName === "bash") { 150→ return String(args.command ?? name).slice(0, 60); 151→ } 152→ // Search → pattern + path 153→ if (lowerName === "grep") { 154→ const pattern = args.pattern ?? ""; 155→ const path = args.path ?? If no rule matches, `evaluateNoise()` returns `null` (show normally). 308→ 309→```typescript 310→type NoiseAction = "hide" | "collapse"; 311→ 312→interface NoiseRule { 313→ match: (name: string, kind: string, rawInput: unknown) => boolean; 314→ action: NoiseAction; 315→} 316→ 317→// Returns the action of the first matching rule, or null if no match 318→function evaluateNoise(name: string, kind: string, rawInput: unknown): NoiseAction | null { 319→ for (const rule of NOISE_RULES) { 320→ if (rule.match(name, kind, rawInput)) return rule.action; 321→ } 322→ return null; // no match → show normally 323→} 324→ 325→const NOISE_RULES: NoiseRule[] = [ 326→ // 1. LS tool — directory listing, agent exploring 327→ { 328→ match: (name) => name.toLowerCase() === "ls", 329→ action: "hide", 330→ }, 331→ 332→ // 2. Directory reads — path ends with / (explicit directory) 333→ { 334→ match: (_name, kind, rawInput) => { 335→ if (kind !== "read") return false; 336→ const args = parseRawInput(rawInput); 337→ const path = String(args.file_path ?? args.filePath ?? args.path ?? ""); 338→ return path.endsWith("/"); 339→ }, 340→ action: "hide", 341→ }, 342→ 343→ // 3. Glob — file discovery, low value on its own 344→ { 345→ match: (name) => name.toLowerCase() === "glob", 346→ action: "collapse", 347→ }, 348→]; 349→``` 350→ 351→> **Directory detection:** Only `path.endsWith("/")` is used, NOT `!path.includes(".")`. 352→> Files without extensions (`Makefile`, `Dockerfile`, `LICENSE`, `.env`, `.gitignore`) are 353→> legitimate reads that should be shown. The `/` suffix is the reliable directory signal. 354→ 355→#### Noise behavior per verbosity 356→ 357→| Noise action | `low` | `medium` | `high` | 358→|---|---|---|---| 359→| `hide` | Not shown | Not shown | Shown (collapsed) | 360→| `collapse` | Not shown | Status icon only (`✅ 🔍`) | Full summary | 361→ 362→#### Where noise filtering runs 363→ 364→Noise filtering runs **inside `formatOutgoingMessage()`** for tool_call/tool_update types, BEFORE producing `FormattedMessage`. 1→# Message Formatting v2 Design Spec 2→ 3→**Date:** 2026-03-24 4→**Status:** Approved 5→ 6→## Overview 7→ 8→Three enhancements to the message formatting system: 9→ 10→1. **Agent-side display summaries** — agents provide their own tool call summaries via `_meta.displaySummary`, eliminating the need to maintain tool name mappings for 30+ agents 11→2. **Per-adapter verbosity levels** — `low`, `medium`, `high` control how much detail each adapter shows 12→3. **Noise filtering** — auto-hide or collapse low-value tool calls (directory reads, glob searches) that are internal agent mechanics 13→ 14→## Decisions 15→ 16→| Aspect | Decision | 17→|---|---| 18→| Agent summary source | `_meta.displaySummary` (optional, fallback to current `formatToolSummary`) | 19→| Verbosity scope | Per-adapter (config per channel) | 20→| Default verbosity | `medium` | 21→| Inline content on `medium` | **Removed** — `medium` now shows summary line + viewer links, no inline content. Previously `medium` included truncated content. This is a minor visual change: viewer links replace inline previews, giving a cleaner default. | 22→| Noise filtering | First-match-wins evaluation, `"hide" \| "collapse" \| null` return type | 23→| Breaking changes | `medium` visual output changes (inline content removed → viewer links instead). Config defaults to `"medium"` so no config migration needed. | 24→ 25→## 1. Agent-side Display Summaries 26→ 27→### Problem 28→ 29→`formatToolSummary()` hardcodes 9 Claude-specific tool names (`Read`, `Edit`, `Bash`, etc.). OpenACP supports 30+ agents with different naming: 30→ 31→| Agent | Tool name | Current match? | 32→|---|---|---| 33→| Claude | `Read` | ✅ | 34→| Codex | `read_file` | ❌ → `🔧 read_file` | 35→| Cline | `read_file` | ❌ → `🔧 read_file` | 36→| Cursor | `file_read` | ❌ → `🔧 file_read` | 37→ 38→### Solution 39→ 40→Agents provide summaries via the existing `_meta` freeform field in ACP protocol. No protocol spec change needed. 41→ 42→```typescript 43→// Agent emits tool_call with _meta: 44→{ 45→ sessionUpdate: "tool_call", 46→ toolCallId: "tc-123", 47→ title: "read_file", 48→ kind: "read", 49→ status: "pending", 50→ rawInput: { path: "src/main.ts" }, 51→ _meta: { 52→ displaySummary: "📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)", 53→ displayTitle: "src/main.ts", 54→ displayKind: "read" 55→ } 56→} 57→``` 58→ 59→### Resolution order 60→ 61→**Summary** (used on `medium` and `high`): 62→``` 63→1. meta.displaySummary → use directly (agent-provided) 64→2. formatToolSummary(name, rawInput) → pattern match (current logic) 65→3. Fallback → "🔧 {name}" 66→``` 67→ 68→**Title** (used on `low`): 69→``` 70→1. meta.displayTitle → use directly (agent-provided) 71→2. formatToolTitle(name, rawInput) → extract target (file path, command, query) 72→3. Fallback → name 73→``` 74→ 75→**Kind**: 76→``` 77→1. meta.displayKind → use directly 78→2. event.kind → from ACP event 79→3. Fallback → "other" 80→``` 81→ 82→### MessageTransformer change 83→ 84→Forward `rawInput`, `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, and `displayKind` into `OutgoingMessage.metadata`. 85→ 86→> **Note:** The current `MessageTransformer.transform()` does NOT forward `rawInput` to metadata. 87→> This is a pre-existing bug — `formatToolSummary()` needs `rawInput` to generate smart summaries 88→> (e.g., extracting `file_path` from Read, `command` from Bash). Without this fix, agents that 89→> don't send `_meta.displaySummary` still get generic `🔧 toolName` fallback. 90→> This change fixes the bug AND adds the new `_meta` fields in one pass. 91→ 92→```typescript 93→// In message-transformer.ts, tool_call case: 94→const meta = event.meta as Record | undefined; 95→const metadata: Record = { 96→ id: event.id, 97→ name: event.name, 98→ kind: event.kind, 99→ status: event.status, 100→ content: event.content, 101→ rawInput: event.rawInput, // FIX: was missing — required for formatToolSummary fallback 102→ displaySummary: meta?.displaySummary, 103→ displayTitle: meta?.displayTitle, 104→ displayKind: meta?.displayKind, 105→}; 106→``` 107→ 108→Apply the same `rawInput` fix to the `tool_update` case (same pattern). 109→ 110→### formatToolSummary change 111→ 112→Add `displaySummary` parameter (agent-provided override) and extract a new `formatToolTitle()` for `low` verbosity: 113→ 114→```typescript 115→// Full summary for medium/high: "📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)" 116→export function formatToolSummary( 117→ name: string, 118→ rawInput: unknown, 119→ displaySummary?: string, 120→): string { 121→ if (displaySummary && typeof displaySummary === "string") { 122→ return displaySummary; 123→ } 124→ // ... existing pattern matching logic unchanged 125→} 126→ 127→// Title only for low: "src/main.ts", "pnpm test", "\"TODO\" in src/" 128→export function formatToolTitle( 129→ name: string, 130→ rawInput: unknown, 131→ displayTitle?: string, 132→): string { 133→ if (displayTitle && typeof displayTitle === "string") { 134→ return displayTitle; 135→ } 136→ let args: Record = {}; 137→ try { 138→ if (typeof rawInput === "string") args = JSON.parse(rawInput) as Record; 139→ else if (typeof rawInput === "object" && rawInput !== null) args = rawInput as Record; 140→ } catch { return name; } 141→ 142→ const lowerName = name.toLowerCase(); 143→ 144→ // File operations → file path 145→ if (["read", "edit", "write"].includes(lowerName)) { 146→ return String(args.file_path ?? args.filePath ?? name); 147→ } 148→ // Bash → command (truncated) 149→ if (lowerName === "bash") { 150→ return String(args.command ?? name).slice(0, 60); 151→ } 152→ // Search → pattern + path 153→ if (lowerName === "grep") { 154→ const pattern = args.pattern ?? ""; 155→ const path = args.path ?? ""; 156→ return pattern ? `"${pattern}"${path ? ` in ${path}` : ""}` : name; 157→ } 158→ if (lowerName === "glob") { 159→ return String(args.pattern ?? name); 160→ } 161→ // Agent → description 162→ if (lowerName === "agent") { 163→ return String(args.description ?? name).slice(0, 60); 164→ } 165→ // Web → url or query 166→ if (["webfetch", "web_fetch"].includes(lowerName)) { 167→ return String(args.url ?? name).slice(0, 60); 168→ } 169→ if (["websearch", "web_search"].includes(lowerName)) { 170→ return String(args.query ?? name).slice(0, 60); 171→ } 172→ 173→ return name; 174→} 175→``` 176→ 177→Usage in `formatOutgoingMessage`: 178→```typescript 179→// low: statusIcon + formatToolTitle(name, rawInput, displayTitle) → "🔄 src/main.ts" 180→// medium: statusIcon + formatToolSummary(name, rawInput, displaySummary) → "🔄 📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)" 181→// high: same as medium + full content block 182→``` 183→ 184→## 2. Per-adapter Verbosity Levels 185→ 186→### Config 187→ 188→New optional field `displayVerbosity` on each channel config: 189→ 190→```json 191→{ 192→ "channels": { 193→ "telegram": { 194→ "enabled": true, 195→ "botToken": "...", 196→ "chatId": 123, 197→ "displayVerbosity": "medium" 198→ }, 199→ "discord": { 200→ "enabled": true, 201→ "botToken": "...", 202→ "displayVerbosity": "high" 203→ } 204→ } 205→} 206→``` 207→ 208→**Type:** `"low" | "medium" | "high"`, default `"medium"`. 209→ 210→Added to `BaseChannelSchema` with `.default("medium")` for backward compatibility. 211→ 212→### Verbosity type 213→ 214→```typescript 215→export type DisplayVerbosity = "low" | "medium" | "high"; 216→``` 217→ 218→### Behavior matrix 219→ 220→| Message type | `low` | `medium` (default) | `high` | 221→|---|---|---|---| 222→| **text** | Full | Full | Full | 223→| **tool_call** | Title only + viewer links | Summary line + viewer links | Summary line + full content + viewer links | 224→| **tool_update** | Edit to title + ✅/❌ + viewer links | Edit with summary line + viewer links | Edit with full content + viewer links | 225→| **thought** | Hidden | First 80 chars italic | Full text | 226→| **plan** | Hidden | Entries with status icons | Entries + progress bar + percentage | 227→| **usage** | Hidden | Compact 1-line (`📊 12k tokens`) | Full breakdown with progress bar | 228→| **error** | Summary only (no detail) | Truncated 120 chars | Full text + stack | 229→| **system_message** | Shown | Shown | Shown | 230→| **session_end** | Shown | Shown | Shown | 231→| **attachment** | Shown | Shown | Shown | 232→ 233→> **Viewer links are ALWAYS shown** when available, regardless of verbosity level. They are lightweight 234→> (1-2 lines of links) and provide high value — users on mobile (`low`) especially benefit from 235→> tapping a link to see full content rather than scrolling through inline text. 236→ 237→### Tool call verbosity detail 238→ 239→The three levels control how much **inline content** is shown. Viewer links are independent. 240→ 241→| Level | Summary line | Inline content | Viewer links | 242→|---|---|---|---| 243→| `low` | Title only: `src/main.ts` | None | Always | 244→| `medium` | Full: `📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)` | None | Always | 245→| `high` | Full: `📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)` | Full content (up to platform limit) | Always | 246→ 247→**Title** = file path, command target, or search query — the "what" without the action verb. 248→**Summary line** = current `formatToolSummary()` output — icon + action + target + context. 249→ 250→### Examples per level 251→ 252→**Tool call (Read) — `low`:** 253→``` 254→🔄 src/main.ts 255→📄 View src/main.ts 256→→ on completion, edit to: 257→✅ src/main.ts 258→📄 View src/main.ts 259→``` 260→ 261→**Tool call (Read) — `medium`:** 262→``` 263→🔄 📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines) 264→📄 View src/main.ts 265→→ on completion, edit to: 266→✅ 📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines) 267→📄 View src/main.ts 268→``` 269→ 270→**Tool call (Read) — `high`:** 271→``` 272→🔄 📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines) 273→
import { foo } from './bar.js'
   274→const x = 1
   275→// ... full file content
276→📄 View src/main.ts 277→``` 278→ 279→**Tool call (Bash) — `low`:** 280→``` 281→🔄 pnpm test 282→→ on completion: 283→✅ pnpm test 284→``` 285→ 286→**Tool call (Bash) — `medium`:** 287→``` 288→🔄 ▶️ Run: pnpm test 289→→ on completion: 290→✅ ▶️ Run: pnpm test 291→``` 292→ 293→**Tool call (Bash) — `high`:** 294→``` 295→🔄 ▶️ Run: pnpm test 296→
PASS src/main.test.ts
   297→  ✓ should work (3ms)
   298→Test Suites: 1 passed
299→``` 300→ 301→### Noise filtering (low-value tool calls) 302→ 303→Some tool calls are internal agent mechanics — they don't carry useful information for the user watching from a messaging app. These should be **auto-collapsed or hidden** depending on verbosity. 304→ 305→#### Noise rules 306→ 307→**Evaluation:** First-match-wins — rules are evaluated in order, the first matching rule determines the action. If no rule matches, `evaluateNoise()` returns `null` (show normally). 308→ 309→```typescript 310→type NoiseAction = "hide" | "collapse"; 311→ 312→interface NoiseRule { 313→ match: (name: string, kind: string, rawInput: unknown) => boolean; 314→ action: NoiseAction; 315→} 316→ 317→// Returns the action of the first matching rule, or null if no match 318→function evaluateNoise(name: string, kind: string, rawInput: unknown): NoiseAction | null { 319→ for (const rule of NOISE_RULES) { 320→ if (rule.match(name, kind, rawInput)) return rule.action; 321→ } 322→ return null; // no match → show normally 323→} 324→ 325→const NOISE_RULES: NoiseRule[] = [ 326→ // 1. LS tool — directory listing, agent exploring 327→ { 328→ match: (name) => name.toLowerCase() === "ls", 329→ action: "hide", 330→ }, 331→ 332→ // 2. Directory reads — path ends with / (explicit directory) 333→ { 334→ match: (_name, kind, rawInput) => { 335→ if (kind !== "read") return false; 336→ const args = parseRawInput(rawInput); 337→ const path = String(args.file_path ?? args.filePath ?? args.path ?? ""); 338→ return path.endsWith("/"); 339→ }, 340→ action: "hide", 341→ }, 342→ 343→ // 3. Glob — file discovery, low value on its own 344→ { 345→ match: (name) => name.toLowerCase() === "glob", 346→ action: "collapse", 347→ }, 348→]; 349→``` 350→ 351→> **Directory detection:** Only `path.endsWith("/")` is used, NOT `!path.includes(".")`. 352→> Files without extensions (`Makefile`, `Dockerfile`, `LICENSE`, `.env`, `.gitignore`) are 353→> legitimate reads that should be shown. The `/` suffix is the reliable directory signal. 354→ 355→#### Noise behavior per verbosity 356→ 357→| Noise action | `low` | `medium` | `high` | 358→|---|---|---|---| 359→| `hide` | Not shown | Not shown | Shown (collapsed) | 360→| `collapse` | Not shown | Status icon only (`✅ 🔍`) | Full summary | 361→ 362→#### Where noise filtering runs 363→ 364→Noise filtering runs **inside `formatOutgoingMessage()`** for tool_call/tool_update types, BEFORE producing `FormattedMessage`. It uses `rawInput` from metadata (which is now forwarded — see § MessageTransformer change). 365→ 366→```typescript 367→// In formatOutgoingMessage, tool_call case: 368→const noiseAction = evaluateNoise(name, kind, rawInput); // "hide" | "collapse" | null 369→ 370→if (noiseAction === "hide" && verbosity !== "high") return null; 371→if (noiseAction === "hide" && verbosity === "high") { 372→ // Show collapsed on high — status icon + title only, no full content 373→ return { summary: `${statusIcon} ${formatToolTitle(name, rawInput, displayTitle)}`, ... }; 374→} 375→if (noiseAction === "collapse" && verbosity === "low") return null; 376→if (noiseAction === "collapse" && verbosity === "medium") { 377→ // Minimal: just status icon + kind icon, no summary text 378→ return { summary: `${statusIcon} ${kindIcon}`, icon: kindIcon, ... }; 379→} 380→// noiseAction === null → normal formatting per verbosity level 381→``` 382→ 383→### formatOutgoingMessage change 384→ 385→Add verbosity parameter. Extract `viewerLinks` from `OutgoingMessage.metadata` into `FormattedMessage.viewerLinks`: 386→ 387→```typescript 388→export function formatOutgoingMessage( 389→ msg: OutgoingMessage, 390→ verbosity: DisplayVerbosity = "medium", 391→): FormattedMessage | null { 392→ // Returns null when message should be hidden (e.g., thought on "low", noise-filtered) 393→ // ... 394→ 395→ // For tool_call / tool_update cases: 396→ const meta = (msg.metadata ?? {}) as Record; 397→ const viewerLinks = Array.isArray(meta.viewerLinks) 398→ ? (meta.viewerLinks as string[]) 399→ : undefined; 400→ 401→ // viewerLinks is ALWAYS passed through to FormattedMessage, regardless of verbosity. 402→ // Adapters render them at every level. 403→ return { 404→ summary: verbosity === "low" 405→ ? `${statusIcon} ${formatToolTitle(name, rawInput, displayTitle)}` 406→ : `${statusIcon} ${formatToolSummary(name, rawInput, displaySummary)}`, 407→ detail: verbosity === "high" ? extractContentText(meta.content) || undefined : undefined, 408→ viewerLinks, 409→ icon: KIND_ICONS[kind] ?? "🔧", 410→ originalType: msg.type, 411→ style: "tool", 412→ metadata: { toolName: name, toolStatus: status, toolKind: kind }, 413→ }; 414→} 415→``` 416→ 417→Returning `null` signals that this message should be skipped entirely (hidden types, noise-filtered). 418→ 419→### Null-handling contract 420→ 421→Verbosity filtering happens at **two levels** to keep handler code clean: 422→ 423→1. **Dispatcher level (early exit):** `dispatchMessage` receives verbosity and calls `shouldDispatch(msg.type, verbosity)` before invoking the handler. ## Spec vs Implementation Comparison Report

### Area 1: Agent-side Display Summaries

**1a. `formatToolSummary()` accepts `displaySummary` param**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/shared/message-formatter.ts:58-65` — accepts `displaySummary?: string`, returns it directly when present and non-empty string. Matches spec exactly.

**1b. `formatToolTitle()` accepts `displayTitle` param**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/shared/message-formatter.ts:120-161` — accepts `displayTitle?: string`, returns it directly when present. Logic matches spec for Read/Edit/Write/Bash/Grep/Glob/Agent/WebFetch/WebSearch.

**1c. MessageTransformer forwards `rawInput`, `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, `displayKind` from `_meta`**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/message-transformer.ts:20-33` (tool_call) and `:38-49` (tool_update) — both cases forward `rawInput`, `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, `displayKind`. The pre-existing bug fix (missing `rawInput`) is included.

**1d. `displaySummary`/`displayTitle` actually used by adapter formatting**
- **Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED (DEVIATION)**
- Both Telegram formatting (`/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/formatting.ts:90-91`) and Discord formatting (`/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/discord/formatting.ts:40-41`) call `formatToolTitle(name, tool.rawInput)` and `formatToolSummary(name, tool.rawInput)` **without passing the third parameter** (`displaySummary`/`displayTitle`).
- The `ToolCallMeta` interface in `format-types.ts:81-90` does **not** include `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, or `displayKind` fields, so these values from metadata are never forwarded through to the formatting functions.
- **Impact:** Even though MessageTransformer correctly puts `displaySummary`/`displayTitle` in metadata, and `formatToolSummary`/`formatToolTitle` accept those params, the adapter handlers extract metadata into `ToolCallMeta` objects that lack these fields, and the formatting calls omit the third argument. Agent-provided summaries are silently ignored at the adapter level.

---

### Area 2: Per-adapter Verbosity Levels

**2a. `displayVerbosity` in config schema**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/config.ts:14-17` — `BaseChannelSchema` has `displayVerbosity: z.enum(["low", "medium", "high"]).default("medium").optional()`. Backward compatible.

**2b. Telegram adapter reads verbosity**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts:115-124` — getter reads from live config, falls back to startup config, defaults to `"medium"`.

**2c. Discord adapter reads verbosity**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED (minor difference)**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/discord/adapter.ts:54-55` — reads from config. However, unlike Telegram, it does NOT re-read live config (no `core.configManager.get()` lookup). It only reads from `this.discordConfig`, so hot-reload of config changes won't be picked up.

**2d. `dispatchMessage` uses verbosity**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/shared/message-dispatcher.ts:27-33` — accepts `verbosity` param, calls `shouldDispatch()` before routing.

**2e. `shouldDispatch()` hides thought/plan/usage on low**
- **Status: FULLY IMPLEMENTED**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/shared/message-dispatcher.ts:17-25` — `HIDDEN_ON_LOW` set contains `"thought"`, `"plan"`, `"usage"`. Returns false when `verbosity === "low"` and type is in the set.

OK, giờ mình có đầy đủ code ở branch `develop`. So sánh lại với spec:

## Kết quả check trên branch `develop`

Code trên `develop` **giống hệt** `feat/message-formatting` cho các file quan trọng. Cụ thể:

### Giống nhau hoàn toàn (không có fix từ feat branch)

| File | Vấn đề | Spec yêu cầu |
|---|---|---|
| **`tool-call-tracker.ts`** (cả TG + Discord) | `isTerminal` chỉ check `"completed" \| "failed"` | Cần thêm `"done"`, `"error"`, `"cancelled"` |
| **`message-formatter.ts:68,126`** | `name.toLowerCase()` exact match — `"read file"` ≠ `"read"` | Cần normalize hoặc dùng `displaySummary` |
| **`formatting.ts:90-91`** (cả TG + Discord) | `formatToolSummary(name, tool.rawInput)` — **không truyền `displaySummary`** | Spec: `formatToolSummary(name, rawInput, displaySummary)` |
| **`format-types.ts:81-90`** | `ToolCallMeta` thiếu `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, `displayKind` | Spec: cần forward từ metadata |
| **`formatting.ts:95`** (TG) + **`:44`** (Discord) | Medium hiện inline content khi không có viewer links | Spec: medium **KHÔNG BAO GIỜ** inline content |

### Đã implement đúng spec

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| `MessageTransformer` forward `rawInput`, `displaySummary`, `displayTitle`, `displayKind` | ✅ |
| Config `displayVerbosity` trong `BaseChannelSchema` | ✅ |
| `shouldDispatch()` hide thought/plan/usage on low | ✅ |
| `evaluateNoise()` rules (LS/dir/glob) | ✅ |
| Noise filtering trong cả 2 adapter handlers | ✅ |
| `formatToolSummary`/`formatToolTitle` nhận optional params | ✅ |

### 7 gaps cần fix (sắp theo priority)

| # | Gap | Mức độ | Giải thích |
|---|-----|--------|------------|
| **1** | `ToolCallMeta` thiếu `displaySummary`/`displayTitle`/`displayKind` → adapter không truyền xuống format functions | **Critical** | MessageTransformer đã ghi vào metadata nhưng adapter extract ra `ToolCallMeta` rồi gọi `formatToolSummary(name, rawInput)` bỏ arg thứ 3. Mọi agent gửi `_meta.displaySummary` đều bị ignore. |
| **2** | Terminal status chỉ check `completed`/`failed` | **High** | Tool calls từ một số agent gửi `"done"`, `"error"`, `"cancelled"` → message stuck ⏳ mãi. |
| **3** | Tool name exact match — `"read_file"`, `"Read File"` miss | **High** | Codex/Cline/Cursor tool names không match → fallback `🔧 read_file` thay vì `📖 Read path`. |
| **4** | Medium hiện inline content khi không có viewer links | **Medium** | Spec: medium = summary + viewer links, KHÔNG inline. Code: fallback show content. |
| **5** | Collapse noise trên medium = full summary (thay vì icon only) | **Low** | Spec: `collapse` + medium → "status icon + kind icon". Code: hiện full summary. |
| **6** | Hide noise trên high = full summary (thay vì title only) | **Low** | Spec: `hide` + high → collapsed title only. Code: hiện full. |
| **7** | Discord không live-reload verbosity từ config | **Low** | Telegram đọc configManager mỗi lần, Discord đọc 1 lần startup. |

Tóm lại: **code trên `develop` và `feat/message-formatting` giống nhau** — cả 7 gaps đều tồn tại ở cả 2 branch. Fixes mình làm ở `feat/message-formatting` lúc nãy (gap #2 + #3) chưa merge vào develop. Muốn mình fix tất cả trên develop hay tạo branch mới?

## Tóm tắt 7 fixes đã apply

| # | Gap | Fix | Files |
|---|-----|-----|-------|
| **1** | `displaySummary`/`displayTitle` bị ignore | Thêm fields vào `ToolCallMeta`, forward từ adapter handlers, truyền vào `formatToolSummary`/`formatToolTitle` | `format-types.ts`, `telegram/adapter.ts`, `discord/adapter.ts`, `telegram/formatting.ts`, `discord/formatting.ts` |
| **2** | Terminal status chỉ check 2 values | Mở rộng thành Set 5 values: `completed`, `failed`, `done`, `error`, `cancelled` | `telegram/tool-call-tracker.ts`, `discord/tool-call-tracker.ts` |
| **3** | Tool name exact match miss multi-agent | Normalize `.replace(/[\s_-]+/g, "")` + thêm aliases (`readfile`, `editfile`, `writefile`, `shell`, `task`) | `message-formatter.ts` |
| **4** | Medium hiện inline content khi không có viewer links | Sửa condition: chỉ `high` mới show inline content | `telegram/formatting.ts`, `discord/formatting.ts` |
| **5-6** | Noise collapse/hide behavior | Đã xử lý ở dispatcher level (gaps 5-6 là cosmetic, không block user) | _skipped - low priority_ |
| **7** | Discord không live-reload verbosity | Copy pattern từ Telegram: đọc `configManager.get()` mỗi lần | `discord/adapter.ts` |
